faq.q: What formats can I convert to?
faq.a: You can convert to JPG, PNG, WebP, and AVIF. WebP and AVIF offer the best compression for web use.

faq.q: Will converting to JPG remove transparency?
faq.a: Yes. JPG does not support transparency. If your image has a transparent background, it will be filled with white when converting to JPG.

faq.q: What is WebP and why should I use it?
faq.a: WebP is a modern image format developed by Google that provides superior compression. WebP images are typically 25-35% smaller than equivalent JPG or PNG files.
AVIF is a next-generation image format that offers even better compression than WebP. It's supported in modern browsers and is ideal for web performance.

faq.q: Is there a file size limit?
faq.a: No. There are no file size limits. Everything is processed locally in your browser.

faq.q: Does this work on iPhone and Android?
faq.a: Yes. The tool works on all modern mobile browsers including Safari on iOS and Chrome on Android.

technicalSpecs.useCase: Best for photos and complex images where small file size matters.
Best for images with transparency, logos, and graphics with flat colors.
Best for web use — 25-35% smaller than JPG/PNG with similar quality.
Next-gen format with best compression ratio. Ideal for modern web projects.

troubleshooting: If AVIF conversion fails, your browser may not support AVIF encoding. Try WebP instead.
Converting PNG with transparency to JPG will result in a white background.
For large batches, conversion may take a few seconds on older devices.
proTips: Convert PNG images to WebP for web use — you'll typically get 50-80% smaller files.
Use PNG output when you need to preserve transparency for logos and UI elements.
AVIF offers the best compression but has limited support in older browsers — check caniuse.com first.
